Partner Applications

Monitoring Gateway Name Change

The PartnerHub name changed to Monitoring Gateway. This more accurately reflects the features and planned updates under this label.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Adapters

Multiple Microsoft Tenants with One CXone Account

Previously, you could only set up a single Microsoft tenant with Presence Sync and Directory Sync. In this release, you will be able to add up to eight tenants.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Previous State Subscriber State for Presence Sync

You can set a new ACD subscriber state in your Presence Sync subscriber rules. This new state is called previous state. When set as the ACD state in a rule, the CXone agent application returns to whichever state it was previously in. For example, Busy for Microsoft Teams is mapped to Previous State for the CXone ACD. In this scenario:

  • An agent's Microsoft Teams state is Busy and they set their CXone state to Available.

  • The agent receives a call in Microsoft Teams.

  • Their Microsoft Teams state changes to Busy - In a call and their CXone state changes to Unavailable - Teams.

  • The agent ends the call in Microsoft Teams.

  • Their Microsoft Teams state returns to Busy.

  • Because the Microsoft Teams state is Busy, and Busy is mapped to Previous State, the CXone state returns to Available.

In this example, Busy is not mapped to a specific state in CXone. Rather, when the Microsoft Teams state changes to Busy, the CXone state will return to its previous state, which was Available.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Voice Quality Metrics

WebRTC Monitoring Available for Salesforce Agents

WebRTC monitoring is available for Salesforce Agent and Agent for Service Cloud Voice. Agents must update their agent applications to the latest version for their activity to be monitored.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Improved Data Organization

Data presented in the Call Details tab was reorganized. This lets you see more information within a single screen and avoid scrolling. Part of this strategy is to:

  • Highlight the most frequently used data by listing it at the top of each column.

  • Make certain pages easily accessible with new buttons, explained in the remaining notes in this section.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

WebRTC Analytics Page

Each contact record in the Voice Quality Monitoring tab has a new page to display WebRTC analytics. These analytics include a variety of in-depth metrics and timelines for the call, such as:

  • Connection jitters

  • Bitrates

  • Packet loss

This page lets you analyze technical details about calls, which make troubleshooting easier. You can pinpoint precisely where call issues exist and where to implement fixes. The Call Details tab of each contact record has a new button to open this page.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Call Recording Visualizer

You can quickly access call recordings from a button in the Call Details tab. The button opens a page that displays each person's speaking time, a waveform of the audio, and allows you to skip around the recording. This lets you validate call issues in the actual recording directly from the Voice Quality interface. You must have call recording enabled to use this feature.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Call Ladder Page

Previously, to access a call ladder, you needed to click the contact ID link then click CXone Voice Diagnostics. In this release, you also have a new Voice Diagnostics button in the Call Details tab to quickly access the call ladder.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Pin Contact Records

You can pin contact records in the Voice Quality Monitoring tab. This lets you quickly access specific records, particularly when handling issues involving multiple calls. This pin is on the Call Details tab of each record, along with the three other new buttons.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Personal Connection

Use Digital Experience Bring Your Own Channel

You can now use the Digital Experience Bring Your Own Channel option with Personal Connection. This allows you to use any digital Any channel, contact, or skill associated with Digital Experience. channel with Personal Connection, increasing the range of proactive outreach tools available to your organization.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Manually Pace Outbound Agentless Messages

You can now control the timing of delivery of outbound agentless messages. This allows you to better manage the quantity of inbound messages you receive in response.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Increased Speed of Hot Leads

The speed of hot leads through the CXone system has been increased. This reduces the time it takes from when a contact invites a solicitation to the moment they connect with your organization. For example, a contact requests a quote for insurance. The request is brokered to five different companies. Studies show that the first company to engage with the contact after this kind of request has an 85% or better chance of closing the opportunity. The increased speed of hot leads through CXone allows you to be more competitive and increase your chances of gaining more business.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Create Regional US Holiday Calling Curfews

You can now create regional calling curfews for the United States. This allows you to be compliant with calling restrictions for state and local holidays.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Increased Support for Active Inventory of Contacts to Dial

Previously, the limit of the inventory of contacts to dial was 1 million. In this release, the limit has been increased to 4 million. 

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Use Cloud Email as an Alternative to the File

You can now use cloud email instead of the file server infrastructure. Opting to use cloud email reduces the load on the file server, which improves stability. This feature must be enabled by your CXone Account Representative.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

CXone Quality Management

Enhanced Evaluations Workflow

  • Supervisors can review evaluations before agents see them. This enables better coaching and provide support to the agents with low scores.

  • Users can only edit their own evaluations. This prevents Evaluators or Supervisors from editing other evaluators' evaluations. To enable this feature, a new permission, Edit Own, is added under Perform Evaluations.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Access Granted Form Templates

Supervisors and Quality Managers can only see relevant evaluation form templates. This prevents errors and makes the process more efficient.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Quality Plan Update

Option to exclude previously evaluated calls in your Quality Plan is added. This helps to represent agent performance more accurately.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

MyZone Updates

  • Users get a true average of quality scores in MyZone for a more comprehensive view of performance metrics.

  • User can access evaluations and calibrations submitted by themselves. It is available in one place for your convenience.

  • You can create an employee evaluation from the Tasks page in My Zone. New Evaluation button is added to perform new evaluation for an agent.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Add Evaluations

A coach can add evaluations to the coaching session in the CXone Coaching app. A coach can leverage evaluation data to identify improvement areas of the agent.

Previously, only attachments and interactions could be added to the coaching session. In this release, you can also add evaluations along with other existing resources.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

BI Report Enhancements

  • Active Status filter and column: A filter by Active Status is added to view evaluation data for Active or Inactive agents. Inactive agents are those that have left your organization. The Active Status filter is available in these CXone QM reports:

    • Evaluation Details

    • Evaluation Question and Answer Details

    • Evaluation Question Details

    A column called Active Status displays each agent's status.

  • Evaluator comments: Evaluator comments are displayed in the Evaluation Question and Answer Analysis report. They provide more context about the evaluation.

  • Contact Number filter and column: A filter by Contact Number is added to view evaluation data for a specific contact. The filter is available in these CXone QM reports:

    • Evaluations by Team

    • Evaluations by Section and Question

    • Evaluation Details

    • Evaluation Question and Answer Details

    • Evaluation Question Details

    • Evaluator Analysis

    A column called Contact No displays the contact number for each evaluation.

  • Skill Name filter: A filter by Skill is added in the Evaluation Question and Answer Analysis report. This filter enables viewing evaluation form answers by skill Used to automate delivery of interactions based on agent skills, abilities, and knowledge. This helps to identify the overall proficiency level for each skill.

  • Sent to Supervisor state: A state called Sent to Supervisor is added to the Evaluation Status filter. It applies to completed evaluations agents have sent in for review. It is available in these CXone QM reports:

    • Evaluations by Team

    • Evaluations by Section and Question

    • Evaluation Details

    • Evaluation Question and Answer Analysis

    • Evaluation Question and Answer Details

    • Evaluation Question Details

    • Evaluator Analysis

  • Expired category: A category called Expired is added to the Evaluator Analysis report. It displays expired evaluations.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Japanese Language Support in Category Management

The Category Manager supports Japanese as an additional language. This helps to create, save, and update custom categories in this supported language.

For other QM processes that use the Category Manager such as Quality Planner and Form Manager, the Japanese language filter is available when selecting a category.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

API Updates

A new entity is added to the Data Extraction API to extract evaluator notes.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

CXone Recording

Screen Recording on Chromebox and Chromebook

Customers can record and monitor agents’ screens on ChromeOS machines, such as Chromebox and Chromebook.

CXone Recording records the screen using a web application. You need to configure application settings. You do not need to download or install client software. You need to configure ScreenAgent to use OS Login.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Enhanced Security for ScreenAgent Configuration

Previously, the ScreenAgent configuration file was accessible to all users. In this release, the ScreenAgent configuration file is hidden by default.

This makes the solution more secure. It limits access to configuration file content, such as Access keys, Secret keys, configurations, and so on.​

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Recording Public Posts from Digital Channels

Interactions of public posts from digital channels can now be recorded. This feature is available for Facebook Public, LinkedIn, Twitter Public, YouTube, Apple Apps Reviews, Google Places, and Google Play. Screen recording and CXone QM are not available for public channels.​

The recorded posts are available for search and playback in CXone Interactions.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Enhancements to Data Extraction API

Version 7 of the Metadata entity of the Data Extraction API contains the following enhancements:

  • You can filter by date and time.

  • The API exposes business data fields.

  • New fields:

    • Associated Contact No List

    • Interaction ID

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Enhancements to Agent Mask and Unmask APIs

The Agent Mask and Unmask APIs allow customers to choose whether to mask or unmask voice or screen recording, or both. This gives customers greater flexibility and control over management of call recordings.

Version 29 of the APIs contains a new parameter, maskRecordingMedia. Parameter values are: All, Screen, and Voice.

By default, both voice and screen recordings are masked and unmasked.​

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

End of Support for ScreenAgent 2.x

From 1 March, ScreenAgent versions lower than 3.0.x are no longer supported. You will need to upgrade all users to ScreenAgent version 3.0.x. You can use the current installation guide for instructions and there are no additional costs.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Reporting

Business Intelligence (BI) Reports

CXone Quality Management BI Report Enhancements

  • Active Status Filter and Column: You can filter by Active Status to view evaluation data for Active or Inactive agents. Inactive agents have left your organization. The Active Status filter is available in these CXone QM reports:

    • Evaluation Details

    • Evaluation Question and Answer Details

    • Evaluation Question Details

    A column called Active Status displays each agent's status.

    Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
    On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

  • Evaluator Comments: Evaluator comments are displayed in the Evaluation Question and Answer Analysis report. They provide more context about the evaluation.

    Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
    On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

  • Contact Number Column: A column called Contact Number displays the unique ID number of the evaluated interaction. The column is available in these CXone QM reports:

    • Evaluation Details

    • Evaluation Question and Answer Details

    • Evaluation Question Details

    • Evaluations by Section and Question

    • Evaluations by Team

    • Evaluator Analysis

    Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
    On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

  • Skill Name Filter: You can filter by Skill in the Evaluation Question and Answer Analysis report. This filter enables you to view evaluation form answers by skill Used to automate delivery of interactions based on agent skills, abilities, and knowledge. This helps you identify the overall proficiency level for each skill.

    Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
    On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

  • Sent to Supervisor State: A state called Sent to Supervisor is added to the Evaluation Status filter. It applies to completed evaluations agents have sent in for review. It is available in these CXone QM reports:

    • Evaluation Details

    • Evaluation Question and Answer Details

    In the Evaluator Analysis report, evaluations marked as Sent to Supervisor are included in the Completed category.

    Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
    On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

  • Expired Category: A category called Expired is added to the Evaluator Analysis report. It displays expired evaluations. Expired evaluations are evaluations that were not completed within the set time period.

    Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
    On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Digital Foundations Tab in CXone Billing Report

A tab called Digital Foundations is added to the CXone Billing report. It displays the number of digital messages sent each day for the time period you choose.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

CXone SmartAssist Powered by Amelia

Amelia Upgrade to Version 6.2

Starting in March 2024, SmartAssist upgraded to Amelia version 6.2. Release notes for this and previous upgrades are available to download:

  • Version 6.2

  • Version 6.1

  • Version 6.08

  • Version 5.6

This feature will be available later in the release cycle.

Customer Request UI Change (in CXone) Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Studio

Unless otherwise indicated, the changes listed in this section apply to Desktop Studio and CXone Studio. If you were part of the Controlled Release (CR) program for CXone Studio, you can find CR-related release notes by selecting CXone Studio in the filter on the top right of this page.

CXone Studio Generally Available

CXone Studio, the new, browser-based version of Studio, is now available. To learn more about this new application, select New Applications from the filter list on the top right side of this page.

This feature will be available later in the release cycle. It will be enabled when the release has been deployed all clusters.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

GET RICH MESSAGE Action

A new action is available for use with digital Any channel, contact, or skill associated with Digital Experience. scripts. The Get Rich Message action returns a dynamic list containing the contents of the rich message. It allows you to send rich message templates that have been built in the Digital Experience Rich Message Settings page. The online help for this action is also available in CXone Studio.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Enhancements to Digital Actions for JSON Payload Support

The digital AskCaller and Txtmsg actions have been enhanced to support JSON payload. This allows them to pass messages containing rich media. The enhancements simplify the sending of rich messages when a single rich message is being sent without additional text.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

ESTIMATED WAIT TIME Action Supported in Digital Scripts

The Estimated Wait Time action can now be used in digital scripts to provide estimates to contacts of how long their wait time may be. The action makes educated estimates based on historical data for a given digital ACD skill queue. This change improves your customers' experience when communicating with your organization on digital channels.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

MCR META DATA Action Supported in Digital Scripts

The MCR Meta Data action is now supported for use in digital scripts to pass meta data to CXone Recording. The meta data must be stored in a dynamic data object.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

UNLINKAGENT Action

The existing Unlink action removes the agent from the call and puts the call back in a post-queue state. This ends the agent leg session. The new UnlinkAgent action also disconnects the agent from the contact in CXone, but it keeps the agent leg connected to the call. The agent can use their physical phone (PBX Internal telephone network that manages an enterprise's incoming, outgoing, and internal voice calls.) to transfer the call to a department with a phone extension that cannot be dialed within CXone. Certain phone types cannot support this kind of transfer, including softphones.

You need to use the Indicate action to give the agent a way to trigger the UnlinkAgent logic. Agents enter the next agent state in their workflow after they unlink from a call. If they unlink before transferring, they need time in a working state like ACW State that allows an agent to complete work requirements after finishing an interaction . This lets them complete the physical transfer before receiving another call in the agent application. Alternatively, you could train your agents to transfer the call on their phone before unlinking it in the agent application.

UnlinkAgent is available to use in any inbound or manual outbound phone script.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

Access Integration Hub through CXone Studio

You can access Integration Hub through CXone Studio.

For more details about this feature, select the Integration Hub filter on the top right.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On deploy This feature is immediately available when the new release deploys to your environment.

CXone Studio

Prior to the deployment of the 2024-1 release, this product was part of an out-of-band controlled release (CR) program following a CI/CD methodology. This section contains only release notes related to the CR program. The changes indicated in the following drop-down were made to the product while it was in controlled release.

For release notes concerning changes to Studio in the 2024-1 release, select the Studio filter on the top right of this page.

Preview Features

April 2024

Subsequent Traces Open in New Window When Initiated

You can initiate additional traces after the first one. The second and later traces now open in separate windows. This allows you to capture multiple live contacts at the same time. You can also run a trace, make script changes, and run a second trace, then compare the results. A maximum of 10 concurrent traces is supported.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Trace Window Titles Show Script Name and the Type of Trace

Trace windows now show the name of the script. They also show whether the trace was started with the Capture Live Contacts option or the Start with Trace option. If multiple traces are run on the same script, the window title includes a number indicating the starting order of the traces. The first trace to run is labeled (1), the second is (2), and so on. This makes it easier to identify the trace you're working with.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Editors Open in View-Only Mode When Script is in View-Only Mode

When the script is in view-only mode, you can open action editors, but they are also in view-only mode and cannot be edited. Action editors cannot be locked or unlocked separately from the script.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Script Changes Automatically Stored Locally

Unsaved script changes are now automatically stored locally in the browser you use. Changes are only stored on the computer where you made the change. If you open the script in a different browser or on a different computer, the last version of the script saved to CXone loads. 

Changes are automatically stored after each script change. If a script closes with unsaved changes, you're prompted to load the version of the script with the changes the next time you open that script. If you decline this option, the last saved version of the script opens. This helps prevent lost work from unexpected script closures, such as when the browser crashes or your computer automatically reboots.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Script Loading Times Reduced

Changes have been made to reduce the time it takes for scripts to load. They should now load faster than they did previously. Additional performance improvements will be delivered in the coming months.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Sequence Editor Support for Trimming Recordings and Other Updates

You can now view the waveforms of prompts you've recorded in CXone Studio using the sequence editor. You can access this editor from actions such as PLAY or Whisper. The waveform shows the sound waves of the recording. In the waveform display, you can select a portion of the recording and replay just that portion. Additionally, you can click Save to save only the selected portion of the waveform.

Additionally, the sequence editor has been updated with some visual enhancements. The buttons under Add Text-to-Speech and Add Prompt now have blue text. The text-to-speech buttons have new icons.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

March 2024

Trace Scripts for Testing and Debugging

You can now test and debug scripts in CXone Studio by running traces. The trace feature works the same way as in Desktop Studio, but not all features from the Desktop Studio trace feature are supported in CXone Studio yet. Currently, you can: 

  • Specify an ACD skill and optional parameters to start the trace with.
  • Simulate an inbound phone interaction when tracing an inbound phone script.
  • Capture the next instance of a script running. This allows you to trace a live interaction.
  • View trace output, including the variables in the script. You can filter the variables list by global, system, and private variables. Additionally, you can view the value of individual variables at a certain point in time.

The following features are not supported yet or function differently from Desktop Studio: 

  • Variable watchlist.
  • Continuous trace. However, you can start multiple traces on a script by initiating them one after the other. CXone Studio can run up to 10 trace instances per script at one time.
  • Save trace file.
  • New variables added by an action are not called out in the trace output. In Desktop Studio trace output, new variables appear in red text.
Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Redact Variables

You can now redact variables in CXone Studio. Redaction eliminates the values of variables and dynamic objects from traces and logs. This is helpful to protect sensitive information.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Add and Edit Action Captions and the ANNOTATION Action on Script Canvas

You can now add and edit action captions and ANNOTATION actions directly on the script canvas. You no longer need to access the script properties to make these changes. 

By default, actions on the script canvas don't have captions. To add a caption to an action on the canvas, you can click the action and click Add Caption . An editable field appears below the action, where you can type the caption . You can also edit and delete captions by double-clicking the action and selecting Edit Caption .

To add an ANNOTATION action to the script, you can: 

  • Right-click on the script canvas and click Text. The cursor changes to a cross and you can draw a rectangle on the canvas. When you release the cursor, the focus is on the ANNOTATION action rectangle so you can start typing immediately without additional clicks.
  • Press T on your keyboard to change the cursor so you can draw an ANNOTATION on the script canvas.
  • Click Text in the left icon menu. The cursor changes to a cross and you can draw the action on the script canvas.
  • Click Actions in the left icon menu, search for annotation, and drag it to the canvas.

You can edit an annotation by double-clicking it. The text field becomes editable and you can make your changes directly on the script canvas.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

NOTE Action Updated

The appearance of the NOTE action has been updated. When you add a NOTE action to the script canvas, it initially appears as a small chat bubble filled in solid black: . After you add text to it, the appearance changes to a small chat bubble filled with blue and some horizontal lines representing text: . To add a NOTE action to a script, click Actions in the left icon menu, search for note, then drag it to the canvas.  To add text to a NOTE action, click the action on the script canvas to select it, then enter your text in the Comments field in the action properties.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Updates to ANNOTATION Action and Caption Editors

The Annotation action editor and the editor for modifying the captions of all actions have been standardized. They now share the same appearance on the canvas. Both editors allow you to change the text that's visible on the script canvas.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Use Exported Scripts in API Calls

You can now use exported scripts in API calls without any additional modification. Previously, some JSON cleanup was required before a script export could be used in this way. To make this easier, the following changes have been made to the JSON exported from CXone Studio: 

  • Removed unnecessary properties, such as scriptName and dirty.
  • Added a scriptContent object, along with xws and yws properties. xws and yws indicate the coordinates of an action's location on the script canvas.
  • Populated the Create Script window with the script name and current location when importing a script file. Previously, this dialog was populated with the script file name.

This makes exported script files ready to be imported through the POST /scripts API.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

February 2024

Scripts Refresh Before Locking or

When you attempt to lock or unlock a script, CXone Studio now automatically checks for a new version of the script first. If a new version is present, it loads the updated version before .

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Record Prompts from the Sequence Editor

You can now record prompt audio files from the sequence editor actions such as PLAY, just as you can in Desktop Studio. You can use the microphone on your computer to record the audio. You don't need to make a phone call like you do in Desktop Studio. When you play the recording back, it plays through the speakers on your computer. The browser may prompt you to grant permissions for CXone Studio to use your microphone.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Text-to-Spech Prompts Read Money, Numbers, and Letters Correctly

The text-to-speech (TTS State that allows an agent to complete work requirements after finishing an interaction) service in actions such as PLAY now correctly reads back sequences A segment of an audio prompt played for the contact. for money, numbers, and letters. When you play the sequence from the action's sequence editor, it reads these sequences as expected: 

  • Money: Reads $42.90 as forty-two dollars and ninety cents.

  • Numbers: Reads 42.90 as four two dot nine zero.

  • Letters: Reads forty-two as f o u r t y t w o.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Action Editors Have Common Appearance

Action editor windows now all share a common appearance. Some actions require configuration that happens in a window that opens in addition to the script tab. These actions have an Open Editor button in their properties panel. Previously the editor windows for various actions had differences in size, opening position, and so on. Now, all editors open to fill the script canvas area. If the Actions panel is open, it closes when an editor window opens.

The options available in editor windows varies depending on what each action requires. For example, actions that allow you to create or modify prompt sequences, such as PLAY, display the sequence editor UI, and the text area in the SNIPPET action editor window provides syntax highlighting.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

January 2024

Override Script Lock from Scripts Page

You can now override a locked script from the CXone Studio Scripts page. Previously, you had to open the script to override the lock. Now you can select Lock Override from the ellipsis menu in the list of scripts on the Scripts page.

Customer Request UI Change Availability GA The feature is generally available (GA) for all CXone customers. Some GA features may require you to purchase a license to use it.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

December 2023

Inactive Scripts are View-Only

Inactive scripts are scripts that have been deactivated by a user in Studio. Now, when you view inactive scripts, they are in view-only mode. Inactive scripts can be viewed by selecting Inactive from the drop-down in the upper right corner of the Scripts page. Scripts can be reactivated from the CXone ACD Scripts page.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Export and Load (Import) Scripts as JSON

You can now export scripts from CXone Studio as JSON. The Export option is located in the drop-down menu that appears when you click the name of the script at the top of the script canvas workspace. You can also load (import) JSON script files. Script files exported from Desktop Studio cannot be loaded in CXone Studio because they are XML, not JSON. Similarly, exported scripts from CXone Studio cannot be imported into Desktop Studio.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Override Locked Scripts

When a script has been locked for editing, it can only be modified by the user who locked it. Previously, there was no way to edit scripts that were locked by other users. Now, you can override the other user's lock. This removes the lock so you can lock and edit the script. Currently, CXone Studio does not notify users when their lock has been overridden. That ability will be added in a future release.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Open Action Editors by Clicking the Action on the Canvas

Actions that have separate editors, such as PLAY, now have a different appearance on the script canvas. They are taller rectangles than other actions. When the cursor moves over the action, an icon appears that you can click to open the editor window.

View Image

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

SNIPPET Action Editor Correctly Handles CASE, DEFAULT, and REPEAT Blocks

The SNIPPET action editor now requires CASE, DEFAULT, and REPEAT statement blocks to have open and close curly braces. If these are missing from a statement, the editor displays an error. Previously, the editor allowed these statement blocks to be created without curly braces.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Tooltips for Errors in SNIPPET Action Editor

The SNIPPET action editor displays tooltips when hovering the cursor over errors. Previously, the error that appears when there's a second ELSE statement in an IF block did not have a tooltip. This has been fixed, and now this error displays a tooltip.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

November 2023

Improvements for Actions that Open Other Scripts

The following improvements have been made to the appearance and functionality of action icons for actions that open other scripts, such as RUNSUB. When placed on the script canvas, these actions now:

  • Have a unique shape. The new shape is a rectangle that's the same width as other actions, but taller.
  • Show two lines of the most important property. For example, they may show the name of the script they're configured to open.
  • Include a clickable icon () that opens the other script in a new browser tab. The icon appears when hovering the cursor over the action icon. If the other script is already open, focus shifts to that script.
Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Action Icons No Longer Highlighted When Opening Script

Previously, when opening an existing script, all the actions were highlighted on the canvas. This required clicking in the white space of the canvas to deselect the icons prior to working with the script. This behavior has been fixed. When opening an existing script, no actions are automatically highlighted.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Text and Icon Alignment Fixed

Previously, in the menu to the left of the All Scripts page, the icon and the Scripts label were not aligned. Now, the bottom of the icon and the text are properly aligned.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Improvements to Breadcrumb Links on All Scripts Page

Previously, it was not obvious that the text at the top of the All Scripts page was really breadcrumb links. Now, the appearance of this text has been changed. The new format makes it more obvious that the text is breadcrumb links that can be clicked to navigate backward in the folder structure.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Improvements to Script Deactivation

The following improvements have been made to script deactivation: 

  • Previously, when a script was deactivated, it still appeared in the list of active scripts on the All Scripts page. Now, when a script is deactivated, it disappears from this page. Deactivated scripts can still be viewed by selecting All or Inactive from the drop-down on the top right side of the All Scripts page.
  • The Deactivate option can be found in:
    • The options menu for scripts listed on the All Scripts page.
    • The drop-down that appears on the script canvas page when clicking the name of an unlocked, read-only script. This option now only appears for active scripts. It's hidden for inactive scripts.
Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Scripts in View-Only Mode Cannot be Modified

When a script is locked, it's put into view-only mode. Any other Studio users who open a locked script cannot make any changes to it.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Errors Related to a Specific Action Are Deleted When the Action Is Removed

Previously, if a specific action caused an error and that action was then removed from the script, the next action added to the script inherited the error from the deleted action. Now, when an action is removed from the script, all errors associated with it are also removed.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Update to Fit to Screen Option

Previously, when the Actions palette was open and the Fit to Screen button was clicked, the actions on the left side of the canvas were hidden behind the Actions palette. Now when this button is clicked, the canvas fits to the space outside of the Actions palette, so that all actions remain visible on the canvas.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Sequence Editor Improvements 

The following improvements have been made to the Sequence editor for actions such as PLAY:

  • Previously, when using the Sequence editor, the cursor did not automatically appear in the text field after clicking the Text to Speech option. Now, the cursor appears in the text field after clicking Text to Speech so users can start typing immediately.
  • Previously, when all sequences were deleted from the Sequence editor and the changes were applied, the action's Phrase property was also deleted. Now, the contents of the Phrase property is not touched when saving changes after deleting all contents of the Sequence property.
Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

ASSIGN Keyword Accepts Values that Start with a Pound Sign ( # )

Previously, when using the ASSIGN keyword in a SNIPPET action, values that started with a pound sign ( # ) caused an error. Now, you can assign a value starting with this symbol to a variable using the ASSIGN keyword.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

SELECT Statement Syntax Improvements

The syntax for SELECT statements in SNIPPET actions has been improved. The editor now requires at least one CASE statement. When multiple CASE statements are added to a SELECT, it also requires a DEFAULT statement. An error is caused if DEFAULT is the only statement, or if multiple CASE statements are added without a DEFAULT.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Syntax Highlighting in the SNIPPET Editor

Code added in the SNIPPET action editor window is now formatted with a syntax highlighter. Different colors are used for commands and keywords, objects, functions, operators, parentheses, brackets, and so on. This makes code easier to read.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

October 2023

Prompt Sequence Manager

The Prompt Sequence Manager has been redesigned. This is the pop-up window that appears when you click Open Editor from the properties of actions such as Play. It allows you to create prompts to play for contacts The person interacting with an agent, IVR, or bot in your contact center..

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Updates to the Appearance of Actions on the Canvas

Actions on the canvas are represented in circle and rectangle shapes. This improves visual comprehension of the script. Support for action captions has been added. The Annotation action has also been added.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

SNIPPET Editor Features

The Snippet action editor has been updated to include the following features: 

  • Syntax highlighting.
  • Auto-completion behavior.
  • Hover behaviors.
  • Error detection.

This editor is a pop-up window that appears when you click Open Editor from the Snippet action.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Dedicated UI Controls and Variable Support for Action Property Fields

Action property fields have been updated. In particular: 

  • Text box and drop-down list property fields of all action properties have been updated so they can accept variables.
  • Boolean, numeric, and array property fields have the option to switch between Expression mode and Value mode. Value mode allows you to select an option from the default field type. Expression mode allows you to enter a text value for the property.
Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Actions Palettes

There are now two palettes that contain actions you can add to your scripts. The Actions palette contains all actions except those that correspond to CXone APIs. The API Actions palette contains all actions that correspond to CXone APIs. You can search each palette independently for the actions you're looking for.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Script Canvas Controls

The script canvas workspace has been updated with the following tools and features: 

  • You can zoom in and out on the script canvas workspace. This allows you to look at the big picture of your script or get close to a certain part.
  • You can undo and redo changes made to the script.
  • Actions snap to the canvas grid when placed on the canvas.
  • You can pan the script canvas.
  • You can copy, paste, move, and delete actions from the script canvas.
Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

View and Edit Scripts in Either CXone Studio or Desktop Studio

You can open scripts in either version of Studio. The script's appearance changes as you move it between applications. The layout adjusts as needed to fit the application the script is currently open in.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Work on Multiple Scripts at the Same Time

You can have more than one script open at the same time and move between them. The scripts open as new tabs in your web browser window.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Suggested Next Actions and Search for Actions from Canvas

When working in a script, you can drag a connector from an action and drop it on an empty space on the canvas to pop up a menu with a search bar. You can search for the next action you'd like to add to the script. Additionally, some actions have a list of the most likely actions to be used next in the script. You can click to add an action from the list. These options make it easier to build your script quickly.

Customer Request UI Change Availability CR The feature is available for CXone customers who are enrolled in a controlled release (CR) program for it. If you're interested in using this feature in CR, contact your CXone Account Representative.
On toggle This is a major release. The feature automatically toggles on later in the release. If you've registered for Service Notifications, watch for one that indicates when. See the Service Notification Timeline page in the CXone online help.

Create, Open, Duplicate, Deactivate, Lock, and Unlock Scripts

You can:

  • Create new scripts.
  • Open existing scripts.
  • Duplicate a script to create a copy.
  • Deactivate a script from the canvas workspace in CXone Studio. To reactivate scripts, you must use the Scripts page in the ACD application.
  • Lock and unlock scripts based on your CXone system's configuration.
